6983ff6febb0dabe802520d3.question: This process happens in sea slugs in the superorder Sacoglossa due to the phenomenon of klepto•plasty. Some types of green and purple bacteria use sulfide or thiosulfate as the reducing agent in this process. An adaptation of this process involves the production of malic acid by organisms in arid environments; that is the (*) CAM [“cam”] version of this process. Algae and cyanobacteria are not chemo•auto•trophs because they produce food through this process, which includes the Calvin cycle and requires chlorophyll. For 10 points, name this process performed by plants that converts light into energy.
6983ff6febb0dabe802520d3.question_sanitized: This process happens in sea slugs in the superorder Sacoglossa due to the phenomenon of klepto•plasty. Some types of green and purple bacteria use sulfide or thiosulfate as the reducing agent in this process. An adaptation of this process involves the production of malic acid by organisms in arid environments; that is the (*) CAM ["cam"] version of this process. Algae and cyanobacteria are not chemo•auto•trophs because they produce food through this process, which includes the Calvin cycle and requires chlorophyll. For 10 points, name this process performed by plants that converts light into energy.
6983ff6febb0dabe802520d3.answer: photosynthesis [accept CAM photosynthesis or crassulacean acid metabolism photosynthesis or anoxygenic photosynthesis or photoautotrophs]
6983ff6febb0dabe802520d3.answer_sanitized: photosynthesis [accept CAM photosynthesis or crassulacean acid metabolism photosynthesis or anoxygenic photosynthesis or photoautotrophs]

6983ff72ebb0dabe802521d4.leadin: After conducting “literati purges” against members of the Sarim faction, one emperor banned an alphabet used in this peninsula to suppress criticism of his regime. For 10 points each:
6983ff72ebb0dabe802521d4.leadin_sanitized: After conducting "literati purges" against members of the Sarim faction, one emperor banned an alphabet used in this peninsula to suppress criticism of his regime. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this East Asian peninsula, once home to the kingdom of Joseon [“JOH-sun”]. This peninsula juts off of modern-day Russia and China and is separated from Japan by a namesake strait.

This Korean ruler established the Hall of Worthies to promote scientific progress. This ruler wrote the foreword to the Hunminjeongeum [“hoon-meen-jung-um”], a treatise about “Proper Sounds for the Instruction of the People.”

Japanese leader Toyotomi Hideyoshi invaded Joseon as a stepping stone to attacking this Chinese dynasty. This dynasty was founded by the Hóngwǔ [“hohng-woo”] Emperor after he won the Battle of Lake Póyáng [“p’woh-yong”].
parts_sanitized: Name this East Asian peninsula, once home to the kingdom of Joseon ["JOH-sun"]. This peninsula juts off of modern-day Russia and China and is separated from Japan by a namesake strait.

This Korean ruler established the Hall of Worthies to promote scientific progress. This ruler wrote the foreword to the Hunminjeongeum ["hoon-meen-jung-um"], a treatise about "Proper Sounds for the Instruction of the People."

Japanese leader Toyotomi Hideyoshi invaded Joseon as a stepping stone to attacking this Chinese dynasty. This dynasty was founded by the Hongwu ["hohng-woo"] Emperor after he won the Battle of Lake Poyang ["p'woh-yong"].
answers: Sejong the Great [or Yi Do or Grand Prince Chungnyeong]
answers_sanitized: Korean Peninsula [or Korea or Hanguk; accept Korean Strait]
Sejong the Great [or Yi Do or Grand Prince Chungnyeong]
